libraryPreparationPreflight.agent_guidance: Use the Jakarta 2.x package names (`jakarta.activation.*`), not the old `javax.activation.*` imports from the 1.2.x tests. No Docker image, environment variable, or system property setup appears necessary.

deterministic_setup.reason: The API jar POM states it is intended only for compiling and excludes implementation-specific classes; instantiated API classes reference com.sun.activation.registries.* at runtime, which is supplied by the matching implementation artifact.

input_evidence.name: issue
input_evidence.summary: #8769 [Automation] javac compile fails for jakarta.activation:jakarta.activation-api:2.0.0; label=fails-javac-compile; body_chars=8015

risks: The implementation artifact also contains jakarta.activation classes, so verification should confirm the tests still exercise the intended API-version behavior and do not mask package-migration mistakes.
The reported javac failure is primarily a javax-to-jakarta package migration issue; dependency setup alone will not fix stale imports.
libraryPreparationPreflight.summary: The resolved jakarta.activation-api:2.0.0 artifact is API-only and has no transitive implementation dependency; normal runtime use of parts of the API such as MimetypesFileTypeMap and MailcapCommandMap requires the matching implementation classes from com.sun.activation:jakarta.activation:2.0.0.
